title: [BUG] Worksheet property defaultRowHeight not works
author: serhiikononenko-valtech
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

Worksheet property **defaultRowHeight** not works.  According to the release history, the similar [bug ](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/634) was already fixed at 1.12.2
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/54435399/133387595-02613078-f3bb-4232-86f8-72bbca44c863.png)


Lib version: 4.3.0

## Steps To Reproduce

```javascript
const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
const worksheet = workbook.addWorksheet(xlsView.configuration.worksheetProperties.worksheetName);
worksheet.properties.defaultColWidth = 60; // applied
worksheet.properties.defaultRowHeight = 25; //not applied 
```

## The expected behaviour:

Default row Height should be applied for the worksheet

Thanks a lot,
Serhii

comments.author: incoming-th
comments.body:
Confirming this is not working on 4.4.0

worksheet.properties.defaultRowHeight

It seems to be set in the properties but not applied

![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/26375917/0fef87e4-20ee-4c7a-a7e7-c5605f159c96)

The only way to make it works is during the construction of the class:

![image](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/26375917/a7bf04c4-bef8-4fc1-a7b3-1bac0e5ab928)

But even doing that will not set the row correctly it will be less than what the number provided, like if you set to 30 it will be 24 in Excel.
